values.name: nTimes
values.type: Int -> a -> Constraint (Monoid a r) a

name: Constraint.Ord

values.name: max
values.comment: Find the larger of two values.
values.type: a -> a -> Constraint (Ord a r) a

values.name: min
values.comment: Find the smaller of two values.
values.type: a -> a -> Constraint (Ord a r) a

values.name: ordEither
values.comment: If we have an `Ord a r` and an `Ord b r`, then we can make an `Ord (Result a b) r`.
values.type: Ord a r -> Ord b r -> Ord (Result a b) r

values.name: ordList
values.comment: If we have an `Ord a r`, then we can make an `Ord (List a) r`.
values.type: Ord a r -> Ord (List a) r

values.name: ordMaybe
values.comment: If we have an `Ord a r`, then we can make an `Ord (Maybe a) r`.
values.type: Ord a r -> Ord (Maybe a) r

binops.type: a -> a -> Constraint (Semigroup a r) a

values.name: eqEither
values.comment: If we have an `Eq a r` and an `Eq b r`, then we can make an `Eq (Result a b) r`.
values.type: Eq a r -> Eq b r -> Eq (Result a b) r

values.name: eqList
values.comment: If we have an `Eq a r`, then we can make an `Eq (List a) r`.
values.type: Eq a r -> Eq (List a) r

values.name: eqMaybe
values.comment: If we have an `Eq a r`, then we can make an `Eq (Maybe a) r`.
values.type: Eq a r -> Eq (Maybe a) r

a -> a -> Constraint (Eq a r) Bool

name: Constraint

unions.name: Constraint
unions.comment:
This type goes by many names: Reader, Kleisli, Function, etc.

This is simply a wrapper around `a -> b`.

values.name: andThen
values.type: Constraint r a -> (a -> Constraint r b) -> Constraint r b

values.name: ap
values.comment: Applies the output of a `Constraint` to the output of another `Constraint`
values.type: Constraint r (a -> b) -> Constraint r a -> Constraint r b

values.name: ask
values.comment:
Threads the input directly through the output.

Due to parametricity, this documentation is worthless,
as there is exactly one implementation of this function.

values.name: bind
values.type: (a -> Constraint r b) -> Constraint r a -> Constraint r b

values.name: constraints
values.comment:
An alias for `ask` that sometimes makes things easier to read.

Due to parametricity, this documentation is worthless,
as there is exactly one implementation of this function.

values.name: map
values.comment:
Transforms the output with the given function.

Due to parametricity, this documentation is worthless,
as there is exactly one implementation of this function.
values.type: (a -> b) -> Constraint r a -> Constraint r b

values.name: pam
values.comment:
Like `map` but flipped.

Due to parametricity, this documentation is worthless,
as there is exactly one implementation of this function.
values.type: Constraint r a -> (a -> b) -> Constraint r b

values.name: pure
values.comment:
Embeds a value in a `Constraint`.

Due to parametricity, this documentation is worthless,
as there is exactly one implementation of this function.

values.name: run
values.comment:
Unwraps the `Constraint` constructor.

Due to parametricity, this documentation is worthless,
as there is exactly one implementation of this function.

values.name: with
values.comment:
Like `run` but flipped.

Due to parametricity, this documentation is worthless,
as there is exactly one implementation of this function.

binops.comment:
An operator alias for `pam`.

Due to parametricity, this documentation is worthless,
as there is exactly one implementation of this function.
